english: Chargrilled Satay Chicken with Jasmine Rice

food_subgroup: Rice-based mixed dish with grilled meat and sauce

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of Kampung Satay Chicken Rice is approximately 450 g, comprising chargrilled chicken pieces glazed in peanut satay sauce served over a bed of jasmine rice with fresh salad garnish.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken satay with rice — composite dish estimate derived from HPB Singapore Foodo database entries for grilled chicken satay and cooked jasmine rice, supplemented with USDA FoodData Central data for peanut sauce and fresh vegetable components
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board (HPB) Singapore / USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single HPB Foodo entry exists for 'Kampung Satay Chicken Rice' as a branded restaurant dish. Nutrient values are estimated by component-weighted calculation: approximately 35% cooked jasmine rice (HPB Foodo: Steamed white rice), 40% chargrilled chicken with satay peanut sauce (HPB Foodo: Chicken satay), and 25% fresh salad vegetables and cucumber/tomato (USDA FoodData Central: raw cucumber, tomato, coriander). Values represent a plausible per-100g composite for a 450g serving plate.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — white rice (jasmine/steamed) GI ~64–72; chicken and peanut sauce components GI ~15–35; composite dish estimated GI ~56 using weighted carbohydrate contribution method
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Kampung Satay Chicken Rice. GI estimated at 56 (medium) using weighted carbohydrate contribution: jasmine rice component (GI ~68, ~35% of dish by weight, contributing ~60% of carbohydrates) and peanut satay sauce (GI ~14 for peanuts per University of Sydney GI Database) and vegetable components (GI ~15). Weighted composite GI calculated as approximately 56. Glycemic load per 450g serving = (56 × 83.25g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 46.6, rounded to 46.5.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from satay sauce and seasoning
hypertension.guidance: A single 450g serving provides approximately 1395 mg sodium, representing 58–70% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg for hypertensive individuals; portion control and requesting reduced-sauce options are advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index driven by jasmine rice and gula melaka in satay sauce
type2_diabetes.guidance: With an estimated GI of 56 (medium) and a glycemic load of approximately 46.5 per serving, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consider reducing the rice portion, substituting brown rice where available, and pairing with the protein and vegetable components to blunt postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from peanut satay sauce and sodium load
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The dish provides approximately 6.3 g saturated fat and 1395 mg sodium per serving; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit frequency of consumption and opt for smaller portions to manage saturated fat and sodium intake within cardioprotective dietary targets.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Kampung Satay Chicken Rice (also known as Kampung Satay Khao) is a Singaporean-Malay inspired rice bowl dish featuring chargrilled chicken pieces glazed in a house-made kampung-style satay sauce. The satay sauce is a rich, aromatic blend of ground peanuts, garlic, onion, red chili, tamarind, and gula melaka (palm sugar), delivering a bold combination of smoky, sweet, nutty, and mildly spicy flavours characteristic of traditional Malay kampung cooking. The chicken is served over steamed jasmine rice and topped with a refreshing fresh salad of cucumber, tomato, and coriander. The dish is a fusion of classic Malay satay tradition with a rice-bowl format popularised in modern Singaporean healthy-eating restaurant concepts. Nutritionally, a standard 450g serving provides approximately 729 kcal, 48.6g protein, 25.2g fat, and 83.3g carbohydrate. The sodium content is moderate-to-high at approximately 1395 mg per serving, primarily from the satay sauce. The peanut component contributes heart-healthy monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fats alongside the saturated fat fraction. The dish contains peanuts and is not suitable for individuals with nut allergies. It is generally considered halal and dairy-free. The medium glycemic index (~56) is driven predominantly by the jasmine rice component, and individuals managing blood glucose should consider portion adjustments. The fresh vegetable garnish contributes dietary fibre, vitamin C, and antioxidants. This dish is commonly found in health-oriented and modern Singaporean casual dining restaurants.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this specific branded restaurant dish. Nutrient values are estimated by component-weighted calculation using HPB Singapore Foodo entries for grilled chicken satay and steamed white rice, supplemented by USDA FoodData Central for vegetable components. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database values for jasmine rice and peanuts. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are high-quality primary sources and the dish composition is clearly described.
notes: 1. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo or USDA component sources used for this composite estimate. 2. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the primary component sources. 3. Trans fat (trans_fat_g) is 0.0; confirmed negligible/trace in grilled chicken and plant-based sauce components per USDA FoodData Central — confirmed zero by component source data. 4. GI of 56 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific dish exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 5. The food_group has been corrected from the input value of 'Vegetables' to 'Mixed' as this is a composite rice-and-protein dish. 6. The cuisine tag 'Vegetarian Friendly' from the input source is not supported by the dish description which contains chicken; this tag has been excluded. 7. This dish contains peanuts and is not suitable for individuals with nut allergies. 8. Serving size of 450g is an estimate based on typical Singaporean restaurant rice bowl portions; actual restaurant serving may vary.
